title: University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Guide for Ghanaian and International Applicants: Complete Application Process, Requirements, and Important Documents
summary: The University of Ghana has streamlined its graduate admissions process to make it easier for prospective students from Ghana and around the world to apply for Master s and Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes. Whether you are a Ghanaian applicant, an international student, or a Ghanaian living abroad, understanding the application requirements and procedures is essential for a successful admission process. This comprehensive guide explains the University of Ghana graduate application process, required documents, eligibility requirements, and important information applicants must know before submitting their applications. About the University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Process The University of Ghana welcomes applications from qualified candidates seeking admission into its various graduate programmes. The institution has established a structured online application system that enables applicants to submit their credentials and supporting documents electronically. Prospective students are encouraged to carefully review all application requirements and ensure that every necessary document is uploaded before submission. Failure to provide the required documents may result in delays or the inability to process an application. Who Can Apply? The University of Ghana graduate admissions portal is open to: Ghanaian applicants International applicants Ghanaians residing abroad Applicants seeking Master s degree programmes Applicants seeking Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes Graduate Programmes Available Applicants may apply for graduate studies across various academic disciplines offered by the University of Ghana through its colleges, including: College of Basic and Applied Sciences College of Education College of Health Sciences College of Humanities Candidates should review the specific programme requirements for their chosen field before submitting an application. Required Documents for Master s Degree Applicants Applicants seeking admission into Master s degree programmes who did not graduate from the University of Ghana must upload the following documents: Mandatory Documents Bachelor s Degree Certificate Official Bachelor s Degree Transcript These documents must be scanned and attached to the online application portal before submission. Required Documents for Doctor of Philosophy Applicants Applicants applying for Doctor of Philosophy programmes must provide more extensive documentation. Mandatory Documents Bachelor s Degree Certificate Master s Degree Certificate Official Bachelor s Degree Transcript Official Master s Degree Transcript Research Proposal Requirement All Doctor of Philosophy applicants must submit a research proposal. The proposal must: Be attached to the online application Not exceed four pages Clearly outline the proposed area of research Demonstrate the applicant s research interests and academic preparedness Referee Requirements for All Applicants Every applicant must provide details of three referees during the application process. Important Points Three referee reports are mandatory Referee information must be submitted online Applicants should ensure referees are informed in advance Referees should be able to provide academic or professional assessments of the applicant Failure to provide referee details may affect the processing of an application. Transcript Upload Guidelines Applicants are required to prepare their academic transcripts according to the University s specifications. Transcript Submission Instructions Scan all transcript pages clearly Combine transcript pages into a single PDF document or one Word document Upload the completed file through the online application system Ensure all information is legible and complete Submitting incomplete or unclear transcripts may delay the admissions review process. Special Requirement for University of Ghana Graduates Applicants who graduated from the University of Ghana between 1996 and the present date must provide their Student Identity Number. Why This Is Important The Student Identity Number helps the University verify academic records efficiently and facilitates the processing of applications. Applicants should ensure this information is entered correctly before final submission. Name Change Documentation Requirement Applicants whose current names differ from those appearing on their certificates or transcripts must provide supporting documentation. Required Document Gazette publication showing the official name change This document must be scanned and uploaded together with the application. Important Notice Regarding Supporting Documents The University of Ghana has emphasized that applications will not be processed without the necessary supporting documents. Applications Will Not Be Processed Without: Official certificates Official transcripts Gazette publication where a name change has occurred Applicants should verify that all required documents have been uploaded successfully before completing the application process. Original Documents Verification Although applications are submitted online, applicants should keep their original documents available. The University may request original copies during interviews or other verification stages of the admissions process. Step-by-Step Application Process Step 1: Review Programme Requirements Carefully review the admission requirements for your intended graduate programme. Step 2: Prepare Supporting Documents Gather and scan all required documents, including: Academic certificates Official transcripts Research proposal (for PhD applicants) Gazette publication (where applicable) Step 3: Secure Referees Identify and notify three referees who will support your application. Step 4: Complete the Online Application Access the University of Ghana application portal and complete all required sections accurately. Step 5: Upload Documents Attach all required documents in the specified format. Step 6: Verify Information Review all entered information carefully before submission. Step 7: Submit Application Submit your completed application through the official University of Ghana online portal. Why Careful Application Preparation Matters A complete and accurate application improves the efficiency of the admissions review process. Missing documents, incomplete information, or incorrect uploads may result in delays or prevent an application from being processed. Applicants are therefore encouraged to: Read all instructions carefully Upload clear and complete documents Ensure referee details are accurate Submit all required materials before completing the application Application Deadline No specific application deadline was stated in the information provided. Applicants are advised to visit the official University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Portal regularly for updates on admission timelines and programme-specific deadlines. Official Application Portal Prospective students should complete their applications through the official University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Portal. Applying through the official portal ensures secure submission of documents and proper processing of applications. GHANAIANS APPLICANTS INTERNATIONAL APPLICANTS Final Thoughts The University of Ghana offers a well-structured graduate admissions process for local and international applicants seeking advanced academic qualifications. By preparing all required documents, securing referee reports, and following the application instructions carefully, applicants can improve their chances of a smooth and successful admissions experience. Prospective Master s and Doctor of Philosophy candidates should begin preparing their documents early and ensure all requirements are fulfilled before submitting their applications through the University s online portal. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover other open admissions on OFY here . The post University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Guide for Ghanaian and International Applicants: Complete Application Process, Requirements, and Important Documents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship Covering 100% Tuition, Living Expenses, Accommodation Support, and More
summary: Applications Now Open for the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026 Students from disadvantaged backgrounds in Vietnam now have an exceptional opportunity to pursue a world-class university education through the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026. Designed to support talented students who would otherwise face barriers to accessing higher education, this prestigious scholarship provides comprehensive financial assistance throughout the recipient’s entire undergraduate program. The scholarship goes beyond covering tuition fees. Successful applicants will receive support for educational expenses, living costs, accommodation, travel, and essential study equipment, ensuring that financial challenges do not prevent deserving students from achieving their academic and career goals. With only two scholarships available, the program is highly competitive and seeks applicants who demonstrate strong academic potential, leadership qualities, resilience, and a commitment to making a positive impact in their communities. Scholarship Overview Scholarship Provider RMIT Vietnam Scholarship Name RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026 Application Opening Date 12 January 2026 Application Deadline 3 July 2026, 5:00 PM GMT+7 Number of Scholarships Available 2 Scholarship Duration Entire undergraduate program What Does the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship Cover? The RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship package designed to remove financial barriers to higher education. Recipients will receive: 1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage 100% coverage of English language tuition fees (if applicable). 100% coverage of higher education tuition fees for the entire duration of the program. 2. Compulsory University Fees Coverage of RMIT Vietnam’s compulsory non-academic fees. 3. Monthly Living Stipend VND 6,000,000 per month to support living expenses. 4. Accommodation Support VND 5,000,000 per month accommodation allowance (where applicable). 5. Home Travel Support VND 1,000,000 travel allowance each semester (where applicable). 6. Laptop Support A one-time provision of a laptop to assist with academic studies. These benefits ensure that scholars can focus fully on their education without worrying about major financial constraints. Who Is Eligible for the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship? To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Citizenship Requirement Applicants must: Be citizens of Vietnam. Academic Requirement Applicants must: Have completed high school. Have achieved a minimum Grade 12 GPA of 7.0 out of 10.0. Financial and Social Requirement Applicants must: Be financially disadvantaged and/or Have a disability. Examples of eligible disabilities may include: Long-term physical impairments. Long-term mental health conditions. Cognitive impairments. Sensory impairments. Long-term medical conditions. Community Organization Referral Requirement Applicants must be referred by a reputable community organization. Examples include: REACH KOTO Vietnam Blind Association Sao Mai Center for the Blind Loretto Christina Noble Children s Foundation HeartReach Smile DRD Local Department of Labour, War Invalids and Social Affairs Other reputable organizations may also be considered. Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed holistically, taking into account applicants’ educational opportunities, personal circumstances, health conditions, and family background. Academic Potential Applicants should demonstrate: Strong academic performance. Evidence of achievement in school or educational activities. Ability to submit a clear, compelling, and well-written application. Professional and Leadership Potential Applicants should demonstrate: Self-awareness and personal development. Clear academic and career aspirations. Realistic and achievable life goals. Leadership experience. Initiative and teamwork skills. Participation in school or community activities. Personal Attributes Applicants should demonstrate: Positive personal values. Active involvement in community initiatives. Commitment to helping others. Personal resilience and determination. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents before submitting their application. Academic Documents Notarized copy of High School Diploma. Academic transcripts. Students graduating in 2026 may submit their diploma later, but it must be provided no later than 18 August 2026. Personal Identification Documents One passport-style photograph (3 4 size) taken within six months of application. Copy of national ID card or passport. Additional Supporting Documents Current Curriculum Vitae (CV). Evidence of English language proficiency (if available). Evidence of financial circumstances. Evidence of disability or health condition (if applicable). Personal Statement Requirements Applicants must submit a personal statement between 300 and 500 words. The statement should address: Intended undergraduate program and academic goals. Career aspirations and future plans. How the chosen program will help achieve those goals. Why the applicant deserves the scholarship. Academic achievements. Extracurricular activities. Community involvement. Leadership experiences. A strong personal statement should clearly demonstrate both need and potential. Reference Letter Requirements Applicants must submit two reference letters. Each letter should contain between 300 and 500 words. Reference Letter One Must come from a community organization where the applicant has been involved for at least one year through activities such as: Volunteering. Employment. Sponsorship programs. Community engagement initiatives. Reference Letter Two Must come from: A teacher, or A representative from the applicant’s school. Both letters should explain why the applicant deserves the scholarship and how they meet the scholarship selection criteria. How to Apply Step 1: Confirm Eligibility Before beginning the application: Review all scholarship requirements. Confirm eligibility. Choose an undergraduate program at RMIT Vietnam. Step 2: Prepare Application Documents Gather all required documents, including: Academic records. Personal statement. Reference letters. Identification documents. Supporting evidence of financial or health circumstances. Step 3: Upload Documents Combine all supporting documents into a single PDF file. Upload the file to an online storage platform such as Google Drive or OneDrive. Step 4: Submit the Scholarship Application Complete the 2026 RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship application form. Include links to the uploaded supporting documents. Submit the application before the deadline. Important Dates Scholarship Applications Open 12 January 2026 Scholarship Application Deadline 3 July 2026, 5:00 PM GMT+7 Scholarship Recipient Notification 25 September 2026 Important Notes for Applicants Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews. Interview schedules may be adjusted by the scholarship office. Applicants whose transcripts do not include a yearly cumulative GPA will have their GPA calculated by the Scholarship Office. Subjects graded on a pass/fail basis will not be included in GPA calculations. The scholarship is highly competitive, and meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee selection. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all scholarship terms and conditions before applying. APPLY HERE Why This Scholarship Matters The RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship is more than financial assistance. It is an investment in talented young people who have demonstrated determination despite facing significant personal, social, financial, or health-related challenges. By providing comprehensive support throughout an entire degree program, RMIT Vietnam is helping deserving students access quality higher education, develop professional skills, and create meaningful futures for themselves and their communities. Students who meet the eligibility criteria are encouraged to prepare strong applications and submit them before the deadline of 3 July 2026. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship Covering 100% Tuition, Living Expenses, Accommodation Support, and More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers, Scholars, and Doctoral Candidates
summary: Researchers seeking financial support to access unique historical collections can benefit from the various grants and fellowships offered by the Hagley Museum and Library. Through the Center for the History of Business, Technology and Society, Hagley provides funding opportunities designed to support scholars conducting research in business history, technology, innovation, politics, and related fields. These grants and fellowships help researchers offset the costs associated with extended research visits while also providing access to a vibrant scholarly community. Whether you are exploring Hagley’s collections for the first time or conducting intensive dissertation research, there are funding opportunities available to support your academic goals. About the Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships The Hagley Museum and Library is renowned for preserving and providing access to extensive historical collections that document the history of business, technology, industry, innovation, and society. To encourage the use of these valuable resources, Hagley offers several grants and fellowships that provide researchers with financial assistance during their stay. Recipients gain more than just funding. They become part of a scholarly environment that encourages collaboration, discussion, and engagement with fellow researchers and experts. Types of Grants Available 1. Exploratory Grants Exploratory Grants are intended for researchers who want to determine whether Hagley’s collections are suitable for a larger research project. Key features include: Funding for a research visit of up to one week. Opportunity to assess the relevance of Hagley’s collections. Ideal for scholars considering a longer-term research project. Can serve as a stepping stone toward a larger research grant application. Researchers who initially receive an Exploratory Grant may later apply for a more extensive research grant if their preliminary visit demonstrates that the collections are highly relevant to their work. 2. Henry Belin du Pont Research Grants The Henry Belin du Pont Research Grant is designed for researchers requiring more intensive use of Hagley’s collections. Key features include: Funding support for research visits lasting up to eight weeks. Intended for substantial and in-depth research projects. Supports scholars conducting extensive archival investigations. Enables deeper engagement with Hagley’s historical resources. This grant is particularly beneficial for academics whose projects require prolonged access to primary source materials. Dissertation Fellowships for Advanced Graduate Students Advanced graduate students pursuing dissertation research may apply for specialized fellowship opportunities. 1. Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship The Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship provides support for doctoral candidates conducting dissertation research. Benefits include: Four-month fellowship period. Dedicated time to conduct focused research. Access to Hagley’s collections and scholarly community. Opportunities for professional development and academic engagement. 2. Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics The Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics supports advanced dissertation research in areas related to business and politics. Benefits include: Nine-month fellowship duration. Extended support for comprehensive dissertation research. Access to valuable archival materials. Opportunities to interact with scholars working on related topics. Benefits of Conducting Research at Hagley Grant and fellowship recipients become active members of the Hagley Center’s intellectual community. Participants may benefit from: Scholarly Feedback Opportunities Researchers can present works-in-progress during the Center’s brown bag lunch series, where Hagley staff and fellow scholars provide constructive feedback and discussion. Podcast Participation Recipients may share insights about their projects through the Hagley History Hangout podcast, helping to increase the visibility of their research. Publication Opportunities Researchers may contribute articles and research reflections to Hagley’s Collections and Research News platform. Scholar Housing Community Recipients have opportunities to interact with fellow scholars residing in Hagley’s on-site housing, fostering collaboration and networking. Academic Events Researchers are encouraged to participate in: Author talks Research seminars Scholarly workshops Special library events These activities enrich the overall research experience and create opportunities for meaningful professional connections. Application Requirements All applicants must clearly address the following areas in their applications: 1. Contribution to Scholarship Applicants should explain: The significance of their research. How their work contributes new knowledge to existing scholarship. The academic impact of their proposed project. 2. Relevance of Hagley Collections Applicants must demonstrate: How Hagley’s collections support their research objectives. Why access to Hagley materials is necessary for their project. The specific resources they intend to use. Important Application Advice Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to contact Hagley Library staff before submitting an application. Doing so can help researchers: Identify relevant collections. Determine whether Hagley materials support their research. Strengthen their applications. Better prepare for their proposed visit. Applicants are also encouraged to review previous researcher contributions in Collections and Research News and listen to episodes of the Hagley History Hangout podcast to learn how other scholars have successfully used Hagley’s resources. Application Deadlines Exploratory Grants and Henry Belin du Pont Research Grants Applications are reviewed three times each year. Deadlines: March 31 June 30 October 31 Applicants can expect a decision within approximately 45 days after each deadline. Fellowship Deadlines Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics Deadline: February 1 Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship Deadline: March 31 Applicants should review the specific fellowship requirements before submitting their applications. Why These Opportunities Matter The Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships provide researchers with more than financial assistance. They offer access to world-class historical collections, opportunities for scholarly engagement, professional networking, and a supportive research environment. For scholars studying business history, technology, politics, innovation, industry, and related fields, these funding opportunities can significantly enhance the quality and impact of their research. Researchers interested in utilizing Hagley’s collections are encouraged to prepare their applications early and ensure they clearly demonstrate the relevance and scholarly value of their proposed projects. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers, Scholars, and Doctoral Candidates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Exchange Program Offering 200 Scholarships for Young Sustainability Leaders Worldwide
summary: Applications are now open for the AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026 Program, a prestigious fully funded virtual exchange initiative designed to empower young people from around the world with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective needed to address today s most pressing sustainability challenges. Organized by AFS Intercultural Programs and developed in collaboration with the University of Pennsylvania Center for Social Impact Strategy, this unique 12-week virtual learning experience provides young changemakers with an opportunity to strengthen their STEM knowledge, develop intercultural competencies, and create innovative solutions to real-world environmental and social challenges. The program is offering 200 full scholarships to eligible youth globally, ensuring that talented students from diverse backgrounds can participate regardless of their financial circumstances. About the AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program The AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program is an immersive virtual exchange experience that combines STEM education, intercultural learning, social innovation, and sustainability leadership. As global challenges such as climate change, environmental degradation, energy transition, and social inequality continue to affect communities worldwide, there is an increasing need for young leaders who can think critically, collaborate across cultures, and develop innovative solutions. This program has been specifically designed to prepare the next generation of changemakers by providing practical learning experiences that bridge science, technology, sustainability, and social impact. Participants will engage with students from different countries and cultural backgrounds while exploring some of the world s most urgent sustainability issues. What Participants Will Learn Throughout the 12-week program, scholars will participate in an engaging and interactive curriculum focused on: 1. Sustainability and Global Challenges Participants will gain a deeper understanding of: Climate change Environmental sustainability Sustainable development Energy transition Global sustainability goals Community resilience 2. STEM Education and Innovation Students will strengthen their knowledge and skills in STEM-related fields through: Interactive learning activities Problem-solving exercises Analytical thinking projects Design thinking methodologies Innovation-based learning approaches 3. Intercultural Competence A major focus of the program is helping participants develop the ability to work effectively across cultures. Students will learn: Cross-cultural communication Global citizenship Intercultural understanding Inclusive leadership Collaboration in diverse teams Bridge-building across communities 4. Social Innovation and Social Impact Participants will explore: Social entrepreneurship concepts Community-centered problem solving Human-centered design Sustainable impact creation Innovation for social good Social Impact Capstone Project One of the most exciting aspects of the program is the final capstone project. During the program, participants will work on social impact presentations that propose innovative solutions to real-world sustainability challenges. The capstone focuses particularly on: Climate action Environmental sustainability Energy transition Community development Social innovation This practical component allows students to apply what they learn while developing solutions that could positively impact communities around the world. Scholarship Benefits Selected scholars will receive a comprehensive package of benefits, including: Full Scholarship Coverage Complete participation in the AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program Access to all virtual learning sessions Participation in collaborative projects and workshops Engagement with global peers and mentors Advanced Certificate Upon successful completion of the program, participants will earn: Advanced Certificate on Global Competence for Social Impact This certificate is jointly awarded by: AFS Intercultural Programs University of Pennsylvania Center for Social Impact Strategy Global Alumni Network Access Graduates will gain access to an exclusive AFS Global STEM alumni platform that offers: Continued learning opportunities Networking opportunities Global connections Professional development resources Mentorship opportunities Future Leadership Opportunities Program alumni become eligible to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, an international gathering that brings together young leaders working toward the achievement of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. Dedicated Scholarships for African Youth As part of its commitment to expanding access and inclusion, AFS has allocated 100 scholarships specifically for young people from: Angola Ghana Nigeria Mauritania Senegal This targeted scholarship allocation aims to support youth leadership and innovation across Africa while ensuring greater representation in global sustainability discussions. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion AFS strongly encourages applications from students representing diverse backgrounds. Importantly: A minimum of 20% of scholarship places are reserved for indigenous and native community students. Refugees are encouraged to apply. Displaced students are encouraged to apply. Students from underrepresented communities are highly encouraged to participate. This commitment reflects the program s goal of ensuring diverse voices contribute to solving global challenges. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026 Program, applicants must: Be between 15 and 20 years old at the start of the program. Have a birthdate between 1 December 2005 and 1 September 2011. Demonstrate interest in STEM fields. Show enthusiasm for sustainability and social impact. Be willing to engage in intercultural learning experiences. Participate actively throughout the virtual exchange program. Program Timeline Applicants should take note of the following important dates: Application Opens 2 June 2026 Application Deadline 2 August 2026 Scholarship Decision Announcement 14 August 2026 Program Duration 31 August 2026 – 22 November 2026 Note: The timeline is subject to change. Why This Opportunity Stands Out The AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program is more than a virtual exchange initiative. It is a leadership development experience that combines STEM education, sustainability, intercultural competence, and social innovation. Participants not only gain valuable academic and professional skills but also become part of a global network of young leaders committed to creating a more sustainable and equitable future. For students passionate about science, technology, climate action, and social impact, this fully funded scholarship represents a unique opportunity to learn, collaborate, and make a difference on a global scale. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official AFS Global STEM Changemakers application portal before the deadline. Application Deadline: 2 August 2026 APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program 2025: Up to $20,000 in Funding for Youth-Focused Nonprofit Organizations
summary: The Carlson Family Foundation is inviting eligible nonprofit organizations to apply for its Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program, an innovative funding opportunity designed to empower local young people to influence community investment decisions. Through this unique initiative, youth leaders have played a central role in designing the grant application process, identifying community priorities, and reviewing applications to recommend funding awards of up to $20,000. This grant opportunity reflects the Foundation’s commitment to supporting organizations that advance equity and create meaningful opportunities for young people. Funding will be directed toward initiatives that address critical community needs in education, environmental protection, poverty reduction, and mental health. Grant Overview The Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program is a community-centered funding initiative where young people actively participate in the grantmaking process. Rather than relying solely on traditional review panels, local youth help determine which projects and organizations will receive support based on the priorities they believe matter most to their communities. Selected organizations may receive grants of up to $20,000 to expand or strengthen programs that positively impact youth and young adults. Key Dates Application Deadline: End of Day (EOD), Friday, July 17 Funding Decisions Announced: Late August Organizations interested in applying should ensure that all application materials and supporting documents are submitted before the deadline. Funding Priorities Applicants must demonstrate how their programs advance equity in at least one of the following focus areas: 1. K-12 Education Organizations may focus on: Literacy programs Academic support services Mentorship initiatives College and career readiness Workforce development programs Educational enrichment opportunities 2. Environmental Protection Eligible activities may include: Protection of natural resources Green space development and preservation Environmental education River and watershed protection Sustainability projects Community environmental engagement 3. Poverty Elimination Programs may address: Stable housing solutions Food security initiatives Economic empowerment Workforce development Financial literacy Access to essential services 4. Mental Health Organizations working in mental health may focus on: Crisis intervention services Wellness and prevention programs Mental health screenings Counseling support Advocacy and awareness campaigns Youth mental health resources Eligibility Requirements To qualify for funding, organizations must meet all of the following criteria: Have an annual operating budget of $1 million or less. Hold an active 501(c)(3) nonprofit designation or operate under a fiscal sponsor. Serve youth aged 24 years and below. Operate within the Twin Cities 7-County Metro area. Not be a current grantee of the Carlson Family Foundation. Advance equity through programs aligned with one or more of the Foundation’s funding priorities. Information Required in the Application Applicants should be prepared to provide detailed organizational information during the application process. Organization Details Organizations will be asked to submit: Legal organization name Organization address Organization website (if applicable) Annual operating budget Employer Identification Number (EIN), unless fiscally sponsored Grant contact information: Name Job title Email address Organization leadership information (if different from the grant contact): Name Job title Email address Applicants must also indicate which primary focus area best aligns with their work: K-12 Education Environmental Protection Poverty Elimination Mental Health Fiscal Sponsorship Information Organizations operating under a fiscal sponsor must provide: Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Fiscal sponsor contact details: Name Title Email address Demographic Information Requested The Foundation seeks to better understand organizational and participant demographics through the application. Applicants will be asked to provide: Organizational Demographics Percentage of staff who identify as Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) Percentage of board members who identify as BIPOC Participant Demographics Number of participants served annually Percentage identifying as BIPOC (optional) Percentage identifying as LGBTQIA+ (optional) Percentage identifying as having a physical, sensory, or cognitive disability (optional) Age Groups Served Applicants may select all applicable categories: Grades K-5 (ages 5-11) Middle School (grades 6-8, ages 11-14) High School (grades 9-12, ages 14-18) Young Adults (ages 18-24) Adults (ages 24+) Narrative Questions Organizations will need to respond to four narrative questions, each with a maximum character limit of 2,000 characters. Question 1 Tell us about your organization’s mission, programming, and what makes it unique. Applicants should clearly explain their purpose, core activities, achievements, and distinguishing strengths. Question 2 Describe the population(s) you serve and how you prioritize equity. Organizations should discuss their target communities and explain how equity is integrated into program design and implementation. Question 3 Describe how you elevate youth voice and leadership in your programming. Since this is a youth-centered grant opportunity, applicants should provide concrete examples of how young people participate in leadership, decision-making, and program development. Question 4 Describe the tools and resources your organization provides to young people that help them thrive. Responses should highlight the services, supports, and opportunities that contribute to positive youth outcomes. Required and Optional Supporting Documents Required Attachment Current Operating Budget Optional Attachments Organizations may strengthen their applications by including: Photographs of programs and activities Outreach materials Flyers Informational pamphlets Community engagement resources Why This Grant Matters The Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program stands out because it places decision-making power in the hands of young people. By involving youth directly in the review and recommendation process, the Foundation ensures that funding reflects real community needs and priorities. Organizations working to improve educational outcomes, protect the environment, reduce poverty, and support mental health can benefit from both financial support and recognition through this unique grant initiative. Eligible organizations serving young people in the Twin Cities region are encouraged to submit a strong application before the July 17 deadline. How to Apply Interested organizations should prepare all required organizational information, narrative responses, and supporting documents before submitting their application. For questions regarding the application process, organizations may contact: Email: programs@carlsonfamilyfoundation.org Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early to ensure a complete and competitive submission before the deadline. CLICK HERE TO APPLY For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . The post Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program 2025: Up to $20,000 in Funding for Youth-Focused Nonprofit Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Social Impact Leaders Across Asia Pacific with Access to AVPN Global Conference in India
summary: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping industries around the world, but for many social impact organizations, the challenge is no longer understanding what AI is—it is understanding how to implement it responsibly, strategically, and in alignment with organizational missions. To help bridge this gap, AVPN Academy, in partnership with DataDotOrg, has launched the inaugural AI for Good Fellowship, a first-of-its-kind leadership program designed specifically for mid-to-senior social impact leaders across the Asia-Pacific region. Unlike many AI training programs that focus primarily on tools and technical applications, the AI for Good Fellowship focuses on leadership, strategic decision-making, and organizational readiness. The program aims to empower leaders with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical skills necessary to responsibly adopt artificial intelligence and create sustainable impact within their organizations and communities. For professionals working in philanthropy, nonprofits, social enterprises, development agencies, and impact investing organizations, this fellowship represents a unique opportunity to gain world-class AI knowledge while collaborating with fellow leaders facing similar challenges. About the AI for Good Fellowship The AI for Good Fellowship is an initiative developed through a collaboration between AVPN Academy and DataDotOrg. The fellowship seeks to equip social impact leaders with the ability to move beyond AI awareness and toward responsible implementation. As AI technologies become increasingly influential, organizations are confronted with important questions such as: How can AI be used ethically and responsibly? How can organizations prepare for AI adoption? Which AI solutions align with organizational missions? What are the risks and opportunities associated with AI? How can leaders build internal AI capacity while maintaining human-centered values? The fellowship addresses these questions through a practical and collaborative learning experience. What Makes the Fellowship Different? Many AI training programs emphasize software tools and technical skills. However, the AI for Good Fellowship takes a broader perspective by focusing on: 1. Leadership and Strategic Decision-Making Participants will learn how to: Evaluate AI opportunities within their organizations. Make responsible and informed decisions. Align AI implementation with organizational goals. Lead organizational transformation in an AI-driven world. 2. Organizational Readiness The fellowship helps leaders understand: AI governance frameworks. Ethical considerations. Risk management. Capacity-building strategies. Long-term sustainability of AI initiatives. 3. Practical Problem Solving Participants will not only study concepts but will also: Work on real organizational challenges. Explore practical AI applications. Collaborate with peers facing similar issues. Exchange experiences and best practices. Learn Alongside Leading Experts The fellowship faculty includes experts from DataDotOrg, including: Lindsey Gottschalk Lindsey Gottschalk brings extensive experience in purpose-driven data and AI strategies, helping organizations harness emerging technologies to maximize social impact. Priyank Hirani Priyank Hirani specializes in AI capacity building and the development of responsible AI frameworks that empower organizations to innovate while maintaining ethical standards. Together, they will guide fellows through the program and provide valuable insights into responsible AI adoption. Benefits of the AI for Good Fellowship Successful fellows will receive several benefits, including: Leadership Development Participants will strengthen their ability to lead AI transformation within mission-driven organizations. Peer Learning Fellows will engage with a network of like-minded professionals from across the Asia-Pacific region, creating opportunities for collaboration and long-term partnerships. Real-World Application Participants will work through actual organizational challenges and develop strategies that can immediately be implemented. Access to AI Expertise Fellows will learn directly from leading experts in data, AI capacity building, and social innovation. Complimentary Access to the AVPN Global Conference 2026 One of the most exciting benefits of the fellowship is complimentary participation in the AVPN Global Conference 2026, which will be held in New Delhi, India. The conference is one of the largest gatherings of social investors, philanthropists, foundations, and impact leaders in the world, providing fellows with exceptional networking and learning opportunities. Global Exposure Participants will become part of a growing ecosystem committed to using AI for social good and sustainable development. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for: Nonprofit leaders. Social entrepreneurs. Foundation executives. Impact investors. Development practitioners. Innovation managers. Program directors. Strategy and operations leaders. Professionals responsible for organizational transformation and decision-making. Applicants should have an interest in responsible AI implementation and a commitment to maximizing social impact. Why This Fellowship Matters Artificial intelligence has tremendous potential to address complex social challenges. However, successful AI adoption requires more than technology—it requires thoughtful leadership, ethical decision-making, and organizational preparedness. The AI for Good Fellowship aims to cultivate a new generation of leaders who can responsibly leverage AI to strengthen organizations and improve lives across the Asia-Pacific region. As AI continues to shape the future, this fellowship provides a timely opportunity for impact leaders to develop the skills, networks, and strategic insights needed to ensure that technology serves humanity and creates meaningful change. How to Apply Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official application portal: Deadline Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. No specific application deadline has been announced at the time of publication. Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Social Impact Leaders Across Asia Pacific with Access to AVPN Global Conference in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative: Up to $800,000 in Funding and Support for AI and Tech Startups Expanding Economic Opportunity in America
summary: The Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation (DRK), in collaboration with NextLadder Ventures (NLV), has launched an ambitious initiative aimed at supporting early-stage organizations using technology and artificial intelligence to improve economic mobility among low-income Americans. Known as the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative, the program seeks to back innovative startups and nonprofit organizations that are building practical tools to help individuals and families navigate complex systems such as healthcare, housing, workforce development, public benefits, and financial services. Millions of Americans continue to face barriers in accessing critical resources and opportunities due to fragmented systems, outdated processes, and limited support infrastructure. Through this initiative, DRK and NLV aim to empower organizations creating technology-enabled solutions that make these systems easier to access and more effective. Why the Initiative Matters Across the United States, millions of people qualify for public benefits they never receive. Others remain on housing waiting lists for years, struggle to access healthcare, or find it difficult to secure better jobs and financial stability. Frontline workers such as: Community health workers Case managers Career counselors Housing advocates Legal aid attorneys often face overwhelming workloads and limited technological support. The DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative seeks to bridge these gaps through scalable technology and AI-powered solutions that improve access and create pathways toward economic dignity and mobility. Funding and Benefits Selected organizations will receive substantial support, including: 1. Direct Funding Successful applicants will receive: $300,000 in catalytic funding. 2. In-Kind Operational Support Organizations may receive: Up to $500,000 worth of operational support. This support can help founders strengthen their organizations and accelerate growth. 3. Access to Expert Networks Participants gain access to: Experienced operators. Investors. Social impact leaders. Entrepreneurs and mentors. Strategic partnerships and collaborations. Together, these resources provide a strong foundation for organizations seeking to scale their impact. Priority Areas DRK and NextLadder Ventures are particularly interested in organizations developing solutions in the following sectors: Housing Access Projects that: Help renters secure and maintain housing. Support homeless populations. Improve housing counseling services. Increase access to affordable housing opportunities. Examples include: Eviction prevention platforms. Housing navigation tools. Software for case managers. Benefits Access Solutions that: Simplify public benefit enrollment. Improve benefit retention. Reduce administrative bottlenecks. Help individuals utilize available support programs. Examples include: AI-assisted benefits enrollment. Medicaid renewal tools. Public assistance navigation platforms. Healthcare Access Organizations creating solutions that: Connect underserved communities to healthcare. Improve healthcare coverage. Assist community health workers. Expand healthcare navigation systems. Examples include: Medical financial navigation tools. Care coordination platforms. Software for healthcare providers serving vulnerable populations. Workforce Mobility Technologies that help people: Access skills training. Obtain credentials. Find higher-paying jobs. Improve career progression. Examples include: Workforce navigation platforms. Career development tools. Employment support technologies. Financial Stability Projects aimed at: Helping individuals manage financial shocks. Accessing tax credits and income support. Reducing debt burdens. Managing benefit cliffs. Improving financial decision-making. Who Should Apply? The initiative welcomes both: For-profit companies. Nonprofit organizations (501(c)(3)). Applicants should possess: A Working Product Organizations should have: A functional product or service. Demonstrated customer demand. At least one paying or contracted customer. Financial Sustainability Most selected organizations will have: Annualized revenue or committed funding between $600,000 and $3 million. A clear pathway to sustainable growth. Potential to serve hundreds of thousands or millions of people over the next decade. Founder Fit DRK prioritizes founders who have: Lived experience with the challenges faced by their target communities. Frontline experience. Strong relationships with beneficiaries and users. Impact Potential Organizations should demonstrate: Evidence of positive outcomes. Strong social impact. Ability to scale solutions nationally. Geographic Eligibility The initiative focuses on: US-based organizations. Organizations serving populations across the United States. Communities with the greatest access gaps. Rather than prioritizing locations with better infrastructure, the initiative places emphasis on areas where the need for support is greatest. Rolling Applications: No Fixed Deadline One of the major advantages of the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative is that applications are accepted on a rolling basis. This means founders and nonprofit leaders can apply at any time without worrying about a strict submission deadline. However, early applications are encouraged, as funding opportunities are competitive. Why This Opportunity Stands Out Unlike traditional grants that provide only funding, this initiative combines capital, strategic support, mentorship, and access to powerful networks. Organizations selected through the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative gain an opportunity to accelerate growth while creating lasting social impact for underserved populations. For entrepreneurs and nonprofit leaders building AI-powered solutions that increase economic opportunity, financial stability, and human dignity, this initiative represents one of the most compelling social innovation funding opportunities available today. How to Apply Interested founders and nonprofit leaders can submit applications through the official website Click here to learn more about this opportunity Deadline Applications are accepted on a rolling basis. There is currently no fixed deadline. Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here The post DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative: Up to $800,000 in Funding and Support for AI and Tech Startups Expanding Economic Opportunity in America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Utah Office of AI Policy Hiring Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher to Shape the Future of AI Governance
summary: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is transforming economies, industries, governments, and societies around the world. As policymakers work to balance innovation with responsible governance, opportunities are emerging for professionals who want to help shape the future of AI regulation and policy development. The Utah Office of AI Policy (OAIP) has announced recruitment for two key positions: Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher . These roles offer a rare opportunity to work directly on real-world AI governance challenges while contributing to one of the most innovative state-level AI policy initiatives in the United States. According to Zachary Boyd, Director of Utah s Office of AI Policy and Assistant Professor at Brigham Young University, these positions are designed for individuals who are passionate about solving practical AI policy problems and helping governments navigate the opportunities and risks created by artificial intelligence. About the Utah Office of AI Policy The Utah Office of AI Policy has gained national attention for its proactive approach to artificial intelligence governance. Rather than focusing solely on theoretical discussions about AI regulation, the office actively develops practical policy solutions that can be implemented across sectors. The office works closely with: State legislators Government agencies Business leaders Technology innovators Academic researchers Community stakeholders Ordinary citizens affected by AI technologies Its mission is to help governments encourage innovation while responsibly addressing emerging risks associated with artificial intelligence. Available Positions 1. Policy Advisor The Policy Advisor will play a central role in helping the office address AI-related policy issues across multiple sectors and industries. Key areas of work may include: AI governance frameworks Legislative analysis Regulatory development Stakeholder engagement Public policy strategy Risk assessment Innovation policy Cross-sector collaboration This role is ideal for professionals who enjoy translating complex technical developments into practical policy solutions. 2. Senior Policy Researcher The Senior Policy Researcher position focuses on generating evidence-based insights that support AI policymaking and governance initiatives. Responsibilities may include: Conducting policy research Evaluating AI governance models Producing policy reports Analyzing emerging AI developments Supporting legislative initiatives Developing policy recommendations Engaging with experts and stakeholders This position is particularly suited for individuals with strong research backgrounds and a passion for technology governance. Why These Positions Stand Out Unlike many policy roles that focus on theoretical discussions, these positions provide direct involvement in building practical AI governance solutions. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to: Work on Real Policy Challenges Team members contribute directly to policy initiatives that affect businesses, governments, and communities. Influence Emerging AI Governance Models As AI regulation continues to evolve globally, the Utah Office of AI Policy is helping shape approaches that may influence broader national conversations. Collaborate Across Disciplines The office works at the intersection of: Technology Public policy Law Regulation Economics Ethics Governance Politics Engage Diverse Stakeholders Professionals in these roles will interact with: Legislators Industry leaders Researchers Entrepreneurs Civic organizations Members of the public Join a Growing National Conversation AI governance has become one of the most important policy discussions of the decade. These positions offer an opportunity to contribute meaningfully to that conversation. Skills and Qualities the Office Is Looking For The Utah Office of AI Policy is seeking individuals who demonstrate: Technical AI Fluency Candidates should understand: Artificial intelligence technologies Machine learning systems Emerging AI capabilities AI-related risks and opportunities Strong Analytical Skills Applicants should be able to: Analyze complex policy issues Evaluate regulatory options Conduct rigorous research Synthesize technical information Multidisciplinary Thinking The work requires understanding multiple dimensions of policymaking, including: Legal considerations Regulatory frameworks Political realities Economic impacts Stakeholder interests Curiosity and Adaptability Because AI governance is evolving rapidly, successful candidates must be comfortable working in areas where established answers may not yet exist. Practical Problem-Solving The office values professionals who can move beyond theory and help develop implementable solutions. What Makes the Utah Office of AI Policy Unique? According to the announcement, the office distinguishes itself by focusing on practical implementation rather than abstract debates. The team actively: Builds policy tools. Supports legislative efforts. Engages with innovators and businesses. Addresses emerging risks. Promotes responsible innovation. Develops governance frameworks. Facilitates multi-stakeholder collaboration. This hands-on approach provides professionals with the opportunity to see the direct impact of their work. How to Apply Interested candidates should review the official job postings for detailed requirements, application procedures, and deadlines. Available positions include: Policy Advisor Apply Here Senior Policy Researcher Apply Here Applicants are encouraged to prepare: An updated résumé/CV Relevant policy or research experience Evidence of AI-related expertise Examples of analytical or policy work, where applicable Because these are core-team positions with significant responsibility and autonomy, candidates should highlight their ability to work independently while collaborating effectively across disciplines. Final Thoughts Artificial intelligence is reshaping virtually every sector of society, creating unprecedented opportunities and challenges for policymakers. The Utah Office of AI Policy s recruitment of a Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher represents an exciting opportunity for professionals who want to be at the forefront of AI governance. For individuals passionate about technology, public policy, and responsible innovation, these roles offer the chance to contribute directly to the development of practical frameworks that may influence AI governance far beyond Utah. Professionals with strong analytical skills, technical AI understanding, and a commitment to evidence-based policymaking should consider exploring these opportunities. For more information on this job visit here Discover more job opportunities on OFY, here The post Utah Office of AI Policy Hiring Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher to Shape the Future of AI Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026: Everything You Need to Know About This Prestigious UNESCO Opportunity for Ocean Professionals
summary: The Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission (IOC) of UNESCO has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026, a structured, hands-on international training initiative designed to upskill early-career ocean and freshwater professionals from across the globe. If you are a government-employed marine or ocean science professional looking to deepen your technical expertise, expand your international network, and contribute meaningfully to global ocean governance, this programme offers a rare and transformative opportunity. This article provides a thorough, step-by-step breakdown of the IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026 what it is, who can apply, what it covers, how to apply, and what to expect throughout the process. What Is the IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme? The IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme is a flagship capacity-building initiative organized by the IOC of UNESCO, one of the world s leading intergovernmental bodies for ocean science, observation, and governance. The programme connects early-career ocean professionals from IOC Member States with specialized host institutions around the world that have expertise in ocean-related subject areas aligned with the IOC mandate. The core purpose of the traineeship is to provide participants with hands-on, practical technical training that they would not otherwise have access to at their home institutions. Trainees are placed at host institutions for approximately three months between September and December 2026 where they work alongside experienced mentors on tasks that directly contribute to the implementation of work plans adopted by IOC Regional Sub-Commissions. The programme is not a scholarship in the traditional academic sense; it is a professional development placement designed for individuals who are already working in the ocean science sector and who need specific technical upskilling to advance the capacity of both themselves and their employing institution. Why the IOC Ocean Traineeship Matters Ocean science and management is one of the most critical fields in the global response to climate change, biodiversity loss, and sustainable development. The IOC of UNESCO plays a central role in coordinating international ocean research, early warning systems, data management, and marine spatial planning. The Ocean Traineeship Programme directly supports these goals by building human capacity at the regional and national level. By participating in this programme, trainees gain: Direct, practical training in priority ocean science areas identified by IOC Regional Sub-Commissions Exposure to international best practices in ocean data management, observation systems, harmful algae monitoring, marine spatial planning, early warning systems, coastal resilience, and more The opportunity to contribute to real, ongoing work plans with lasting regional impact Membership in a growing alumni network of ocean traineeship graduates International recognition from UNESCO and the IOC A final platform to present their learning outcomes to all mentors and trainees worldwide Programme Timeline The 2026 IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme operates on the following schedule: Host Institution Call for Applications: Closed (deadline was extended to 17 May 2026) Trainee Call for Applications: Open from 19 June to 5 July 2026 Traineeship Period: September to December 2026 (approximately 3 months) Final Online Presentation: 2027 (after all trainees complete their placements) The application window for trainees is narrow only open from 19 June to 5 July 2026 so prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their documents well in advance and submit before the deadline. The 8 Host Institutions for 2026 For the 2026 cohort, there are eight host institutions offering placements across multiple continents and ocean science specializations. Each institution operates in a specific language and is linked to a particular IOC programme area. Applicants may only choose one host institution, so it is essential to carefully assess which institution best aligns with your professional goals and current work. 1. Phycology Division, Natural Sciences Facility, National University of La Plata, Argentina Website: https://www.fcnym.unlp.edu.ar/ Languages: Spanish or English IOC Programme Area: Ocean Science, Harmful Algae Bloom 2. Universidade Federal do Espirito Santo, Brazil Website: http://www.ufes.br/ Languages: Portuguese or English IOC Programme Area: Biodiversity Data Management 3. Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS), India Website: https://www.incois.gov.in Language: English IOC Programme Area: Ocean Observation System 4. Spanish Institute of Oceanography (IEO), Spain Website: https://ieo.csic.es/ Languages: Spanish or English IOC Programme Area: Oceanographic Data Management 5. National Hydrographic Agency, Nigeria Website: https://nha.gov.ng/ Language: English IOC Programme Area: Ocean Observation System 6. Second Institute of Oceanography, Ministry of Natural Resources, China Website: https://www.sio.org.cn/ Language: English IOC Programme Area: Early Warning Systems, Coastal Resilience, Data Management 7. Agency for Meteorology, Climatology and Geophysics of the Republic of Indonesia (BMKG) Website: http://www.bmkg.go.id/ Language: English IOC Programme Area: Early Warning and Hazards 8. The Pacific Community (SPC), Fiji Website: https://www.spc.int/ Language: English IOC Programme Area: Marine Spatial Planning The diversity of host institutions spanning South America, South Asia, Europe, Africa, East Asia, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific reflects the IOC s commitment to geographic balance and inclusive capacity building across all ocean regions. Eligibility Criteria for Trainee Applicants Before applying, candidates must ensure they meet all of the following requirements: Be at the early stages of career development in the ocean or marine science sector Be a citizen of an IOC Member State Be currently employed by a government institution Have a guarantee of remaining employed by that government institution for a minimum of one year after the traineeship concludes Be actively working on activities that contribute to the implementation of IOC Regional Sub-Commission work plans or the broader IOC mandate Be able to provide a supporting document and recommendation letter from their supervisor or employing institution Possess sufficient language skills in English, Spanish, or Portuguese, depending on the chosen host institution (language proficiency will be tested) Upload a current curriculum vitae (CV) It is important to note that incomplete applications will not be considered. Applicants are expected to submit all required documentation in full before the deadline of 5 July 2026 . What the IOC Covers: Financial Support for Trainees The IOC Ocean Traineeship is not a salaried position, but it comes with meaningful financial support that makes international placement feasible for eligible candidates. Successful trainees will receive: Round-trip international airfare from the trainee s country of residence to the nearest airport to the host institution A monthly stipend to cover living expenses during the training period, calculated based on recommendations from the host institution Travel insurance for the duration of the placement Applicants should note, however, that the following costs are not covered and must be arranged independently: Local/domestic transport at the host location Visa application fees and related documentation costs Medical tests required for travel or visa purposes Any other personal domestic expenses Trainees are encouraged to contact the programme organizers early to discuss expected expenses with the relevant host institution before submitting their application. The Application Process: Step by Step Applying to the IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme requires careful preparation. Here is what the process looks like for trainee applicants: Step 1: Choose Your Host Institution Review the list of eight host institutions carefully. Consider the language of instruction, the IOC programme area, and how the training aligns with your current role and your employer s needs. Remember, you may only apply to one host institution. Step 2: Prepare Your Application Documents Gather the following: A completed online application form Your updated CV A recommendation/supporting letter from your supervisor at your employing institution A justification explaining how the chosen host institution aligns with your traineeship objectives and how the training will benefit your employing institution Step 3: Submit Before the Deadline The call for trainee applications is open from 19 June to 5 July 2026 . Submit your completed application via the online application form before the deadline. Incomplete applications will be disqualified. Step 4: Interview (If Shortlisted) If your application is shortlisted, you will be invited to participate in an interview with the IOC secretariat and the host supervisor/mentor. During this interview, you will collaboratively design the work plan for your traineeship. Step 5: Complete the Traineeship Successful candidates will undertake their placement between September and December 2026, for approximately three months. How Applications Are Evaluated The IOC of UNESCO and the host institutions will evaluate applications based on five criteria: The applicant s professional profile and background The overall quality and completeness of the application The relevance of the application to IOC Regional Sub-Commission workplan priorities The justification of how the training will build lasting capacity for the individual and their employing organization at the regional level Geographic and gender balance among selected trainees This last criterion is especially significant the IOC is committed to ensuring that trainees are selected from a diverse range of regions and that gender balance is maintained across the cohort. Link to the application form. Post-Traineeship Obligations and Alumni Network The IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme does not end when the placement concludes. Trainees are expected to fulfil the following after completing their time at the host institution: Submit a final report providing insights and reflections on the traineeship experience and activities Participate in an evaluation session with their mentor, the results of which are to be shared with the IOC secretariat Complete a feedback survey about the programme Share photographs documenting their traineeship experience Deliver an online presentation in 2027 to all mentors and trainees, sharing the knowledge gained, challenges encountered, and how the skills are being implemented at their home institution In addition, all graduates of the traineeship will be inducted into the IOC Ocean Traineeship Alumni Network, a growing community of ocean professionals who have completed the programme and continue to contribute to the IOC s global mission. Contact and Additional Information For any questions regarding the IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026, prospective applicants are encouraged to contact the programme team at: Email: s.de-baenst@unesco.org Programme organized through the UNESCO/IOC Project Office for IODE, InnovOcean Campus, Jacobsenstraat 1, 8400 Oostende, Belgium An online information session can also be arranged upon request. Visit HERE to learn more about the IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026. Conclusion The IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026 is a genuinely exceptional opportunity for early-career government-employed ocean professionals from IOC Member States who are looking to gain hands-on international experience, build specialized technical skills, and contribute to the global ocean science agenda under the banner of UNESCO. With placements available at eight leading institutions across Argentina, Brazil, India, Spain, Nigeria, China, Indonesia, and Fiji, and with full coverage of international airfare, a monthly stipend, and travel insurance, the programme removes many of the financial barriers that typically prevent professionals from developing nations from accessing world-class training. The application window for trainees is tight open only from 19 June to 5 July 2026 making it imperative that eligible candidates act swiftly and submit complete, well-prepared applications. Whether your area of focus is harmful algae monitoring, ocean observation systems, biodiversity data management, early warning systems, coastal resilience, or marine spatial planning, there is a host institution in this programme that can take your expertise to the next level. Do not miss this chance to elevate your career, serve your institution, and contribute to the health of our global ocean. Discover more global Internship and Traineeship opportunities on OFY. The post IOC Ocean Traineeship Programme 2026: Everything You Need to Know About This Prestigious UNESCO Opportunity for Ocean Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Training on Litigation of Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights in Africa 2026 | Fully Funded Legal Capacity-Building Opportunity in Kenya
summary: ​ The Centre for Human Rights at the University of Pretoria is inviting applications for an intensive regional training programme focused on strengthening the ability of legal professionals and human rights advocates to advance sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) through strategic litigation. The Training on Litigation of Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights Before National and Regional Mechanisms in Africa is designed to equip participants with practical skills needed to use legal systems, courts, and human rights mechanisms to promote justice, accountability, and protection of rights across Africa. The two-day residential training will bring together legal practitioners, human rights defenders, researchers, civil society actors, and adjudicators working in the field of SRHR. Successful applicants will receive full support covering participation-related expenses, including travel, accommodation, meals, and other approved costs. Application Deadline: Wednesday, 1 July 2026 Training Dates: 3–4 September 2026 About the Training Programme The Centre for Human Rights, Faculty of Law at the University of Pretoria, is organising this capacity-building programme as part of its ongoing commitment to strengthening: Sexual and reproductive health and rights advocacy Human rights accountability Strategic litigation Access to justice Legal protection for vulnerable communities The training responds to the continuing gap between awareness of SRHR standards and the practical ability to use litigation and legal mechanisms to secure meaningful change. Although important progress has been made in recognising SRHR protections, many individuals and communities continue to face barriers in accessing justice and enforcing their rights. Training Location and Schedule Training Location: Nairobi, Kenya Dates: Arrival: 2 September 2026 Training sessions: 3–4 September 2026 Departure: 5 September 2026 The programme will be delivered as a residential training, allowing participants to engage deeply with facilitators, experts, and fellow advocates. Funding and Support for Selected Participants The Centre for Human Rights will cover participation costs for successful applicants. Support includes: Economy-class travel ticket Accommodation Visa-related costs Meals during the training period Airport pick-up and drop-off Other approved participation-related expenses Participants will therefore be able to attend without covering the major costs associated with participation. Purpose of the Training The programme is designed to move beyond general awareness and provide practical litigation skills that participants can apply in their professional work. The training aims to strengthen participants’ ability to: Identify strategic SRHR cases Develop effective litigation strategies Use national and regional human rights mechanisms Engage with courts and accountability institutions Advocate for implementation of legal decisions and remedies Key Areas Covered During the Training Participants will explore practical approaches to SRHR litigation, including: 1. Strategic Case Identification Participants will learn how to: Identify cases with potential legal and social impact Assess opportunities for strategic litigation Build stronger legal arguments around SRHR violations 2. Litigation Strategy Development The training will strengthen skills in: Preparing litigation approaches Developing advocacy strategies Using evidence effectively Connecting legal arguments with human rights frameworks 3. National and Regional Human Rights Mechanisms Participants will gain knowledge on: Domestic legal systems Regional African human rights mechanisms Accountability pathways Using legal institutions to advance SRHR protections 4. Supporting Justice Outcomes The programme will also focus on: Monitoring implementation of decisions Strengthening advocacy after court judgments Ensuring legal victories translate into practical improvements Why This Training Is Important Across Africa, many communities continue to experience challenges accessing sexual and reproductive health rights protections. The training recognises the need to strengthen legal action for groups facing discrimination and exclusion, including: Women and girls Adolescents and young people Persons with disabilities LGBTIQ+ persons Sex workers People living in rural and underserved communities Other groups experiencing intersecting forms of discrimination By improving litigation capacity, participants can contribute to stronger human rights protections and more effective accountability systems. Who Should Apply? Applications are encouraged from professionals and advocates who are actively engaged in SRHR-related work. Eligible participants include: Lawyers Human rights litigators Civil society organisations Community advocates Researchers Legal support professionals Judges or adjudicators involved in SRHR matters Applicants should demonstrate how the training will: Strengthen their professional capacity Improve their organisation’s work Benefit communities they serve Support stronger SRHR advocacy outcomes Application Requirements Applicants must show: Current involvement in SRHR-related litigation or advocacy Relevant professional experience Interest in strengthening legal accountability Potential impact of applying the training within their organisation or community Because spaces are limited, applicants are encouraged to submit strong applications explaining their motivation and expected impact. Selection Timeline Important dates: Application Deadline: 1 July 2026 Selection Notification: 17 July 2026 Training Dates: 3–4 September 2026 How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Interested applicants should complete the application form through the official programme application portal before the deadline. This training provides a valuable opportunity for African legal professionals and human rights advocates to strengthen their ability to use law as a tool for justice, equality, and social change. Click here for more global training opportunities The post Training on Litigation of Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights in Africa 2026 | Fully Funded Legal Capacity-Building Opportunity in Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
